displayTitle: The Coil Reads, the Standard Decides

beats.narration: Eddy current testing never touches the part. A coil induces currents in the metal, and the instrument watches how those currents push back.

payload.title: THE COIL READS, THE STANDARD DECIDES
payload.sub: Sensors, equipment, and the reference that gives a signal meaning.
payload.panelLabel: ET PROBE // THE COIL IS THE SENSOR

beats.narration: The sensor is almost always a coil, shaped to put currents where flaws hide: surface probes for flat work, bobbins inside tubing, encircling coils around mill product.
Wire it absolute and one coil reads everything, gradual or abrupt. Wire it differential and two opposed coils cancel what they share, so a sharp local flaw jumps out.
Equipment ranges from go, no-go crack detectors to impedance-plane displays. The flying dot shows phase and amplitude together: phase tells you what, amplitude how much.

payload.heading: From a needle to the impedance plane
payload.note: PHASE = WHAT IT IS // AMPLITUDE = HOW SEVERE

beats.narration: But every reading is relative. Until you standardize on a reference of the same alloy, temper, and size, with machined notches of known depth, a deflection is just a wiggle.

payload.sub: No reference standard, no test — re-check it or yesterday's calls are suspect.
